# QuilrAI Platform Documentation

> QuilrAI helps security, governance, and IT teams discover AI usage, understand risk, and safely roll out controls across users, apps, accounts, endpoints, gateways, and integrations.

## Overview

- [Platform Overview](https://platform-docs.quilrai.dev/): Customer-facing overview of the QuilrAI platform, including Insights, Findings, Users, Applications, Accounts, AI Inventory, Controls, Detection Models, AI Gateway, MCP Gateway, Browser Extension, Endpoint Agent, Integrations, Settings, Audit Log, and Exports.

## Key Platform Areas

- Insights: AI adoption trends, top apps, top categories, top users, sensitive prompts, and department usage.
- Findings: Summary insights and investigation views across browser extension, endpoint agent, LLM Gateway, MCP Gateway, identity, and all findings.
- Users, Applications, and Accounts: Entity-level usage, risk, conversations, drilldowns, app intelligence, and findings links.
- AI Inventory: SaaS and endpoint AI asset inventory, including models, agents, RAG systems, datasets, guardrails, tags, and adoption trends.
- Controls: Policy and playbook management by posture, with system and custom controls, monitor/action modes, and enable/disable workflows.
- Detection Models: Out-of-box and custom data-risk detections, contextual and non-contextual risks, and precision, semantic, and intent techniques.
- AI Gateway: LLM Gateway and MCP Gateway configuration, logs, guardrails, access control, routing, red teaming, tools, and agent mappings.
- Browser Extension and Endpoint Agent: Deployment, status, management, whitelisting, detection configuration, DLP actions, and browser monitoring controls.
- Integrations: Connected and available connectors, instance configuration, and integration guidance for Okta, Jamf, ChatGPT, and AWS.
- Settings and Administration: Organizational context, policies, smart groups, user management, interaction customization, compliance setup, and gateway setup.
- Audit Log and Exports: Searchable audit history, event snapshots, export history, and CSV or JSON exports.
